skill.name: reasoning-analogical
skill.description: Transfer knowledge from source domains to novel target situations through structural mapping. Use when facing new markets, products, or situations where past experience provides relevant patterns. Produces adapted solutions with explicit mappings and context adjustments.
skill.summary: Apply analogical reasoning to map prior experience onto new markets, products, and strategic situations.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found no executable code or malicious behavior. The hits are false positives from Markdown code fences, sample YAML, type-signature notation, and ordinary prose in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer interpreted Markdown backtick fences and example blocks as Ruby or shell execution. The referenced lines are documentation examples, not executable commands, and no command invocation is requested.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations are Markdown fences or example output boundaries. I found no command syntax, no user input execution path, and no instruction to run shell commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at prose locations. Line 3 is the skill description, and line 283 starts a documentation section about common reasoning failures; neither uses or recommends cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no cryptographic API, hash algorithm, secret handling, or encoded payload. The duplicate line 3 finding appears to be a scanner match on non-security text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported system reconnaissance at line 49, but the line is a table row about success level in source selection. It does not request host, user, process, network, or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is ordinary methodology text. I found no reconnaissance command, no system data collection request, and no surrounding malicious context.

content.user_title: Map Past Experience to New Problems
content.value_statement: Teams often overfit old playbooks or ignore useful prior experience when entering new situations. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured analogy, context mapping, and adapted action plans.
actual_capabilities: Selects relevant source cases using similarity, outcome quality, recency, and context overlap.
Maps objects, relations, mechanisms, constraints, and success factors across domains.
Separates preserved, modified, broken, and new elements in the target context.
Builds adapted execution plans with phases, timeline, milestones, confidence, and uncertainty.
Applies quality gates for source quality, mapping coverage, specificity, and confidence.
limitations: It depends on accurate source cases and documented outcomes from the user or workspace.
It does not verify market facts, metrics, or platform capabilities by itself.
It can produce weak recommendations when source and target structures are only superficially similar.
It is a reasoning framework, not an execution engine or automated research tool.

use_cases.title: Plan a Market Entry
use_cases.description: Compare a successful prior launch with a new market and adapt the acquisition, activation, and retention plan.

use_cases.title: Adapt a Product Playbook
use_cases.description: Translate product lessons from one customer segment into a new segment while marking what breaks or changes.

use_cases.title: Review Strategic Assumptions
use_cases.description: Expose where a team is copying past success too directly and identify context differences that need new work.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Useful Analogy
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with one new problem and several past examples.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use analogical reasoning for this new situation: [target]. Consider these past cases: [sources]. Select the best source and explain why.

prompt_templates.title: Map Source to Target
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use after a likely source case is known.
prompt_templates.prompt: Map this source case to my target context. Identify objects, relations, mechanisms, constraints, preserved elements, modified elements, and broken elements.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt a Launch Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning use for market, product, or operational transfer.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the analogical reasoning process to adapt this proven playbook to a new context. Include context differences, execution phases, timeline, confidence, and uncertainty.

prompt_templates.title: Stress Test the Analogy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use before committing to a strategic decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Critique this analogy before I use it for planning. Find surface similarities, over-transfer risks, missing adaptations, weak source evidence, and assumptions that need testing.

output_examples.input: Expand a fashion recommendation product into home goods using past fashion launch experience.
output_examples.output: The output identifies fashion DTC as the source, maps fit concerns to dimension concerns, marks room measurement as a new requirement, and proposes a phased home goods pilot.

output_examples.input: Adapt a self-serve app store launch to an assisted B2B marketplace launch.
output_examples.output: The output preserves integration-first positioning, changes self-serve onboarding into assisted sales, replaces reviews with case studies, and flags longer buying cycles.

output_examples.input: Check whether a prior marketplace playbook applies to a new regulated platform.
output_examples.output: The output separates transferable marketplace mechanics from policy constraints, lists broken assumptions, and recommends validation steps before launch.

best_practices: Provide at least two candidate source cases with outcomes and metrics.
Ask for preserved, modified, broken, and new elements before asking for a plan.
Use the confidence and uncertainty sections to decide what needs validation next.
anti_patterns: Do not use surface similarity alone as proof that a playbook transfers.
Do not skip context differences when source and target look familiar.
Do not treat the adapted plan as validated market evidence.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps transfer lessons from known cases to new situations through explicit structural mapping and adaptation.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown reasoning framework and does not include executable scripts or file access instructions.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the target situation, candidate source cases, known outcomes, constraints, and any metrics that show what worked.

faq.question: Can it choose between multiple source cases?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes criteria for structural similarity, documented outcomes, recency, success level, and context overlap.

faq.question: How does it prevent bad analogies?
faq.answer: It requires explicit mapping, context difference analysis, confidence scoring, and checks for over-transfer or surface similarity.

faq.question: Is this a research tool?
faq.answer: No. It organizes reasoning from supplied information, but it does not independently verify facts or gather market data.
